body: Dense pass 2 done (WARRANT#92). Two investigations: (1) Checked whether P3B's holdout/OOS windows were needlessly small -- DISPROVEN by reading the actual code: it already uses ~90% of all available history (59827/66095 bars), matching the live log exactly. More samples per regime would need more broker history (a terminal setting, not code) or a coarser regime design -- not a bug. (2) Found something real: the 'enh-check' block claimed to test candidate weights against the true 14-layer live scorer but structurally could not -- it was re-testing the SAME linear scorer on a different sample. That mislabeling is WHY I misread the evidence in my last report. Fixed the labels (enh-check -> resample-check etc), zero computation changed, compiled clean. Bottom line, stated plainly: I did not double the stats and did not fake it. Real WR improvement needs one of -- more history, a real (higher-risk) enhanced-scorer refactor, or a multiple-testing correction on the 562,500-combo weight search (flagged as the most promising concrete next step, unfixed since 2026-07-17 per the code's own comment). All three are real follow-on warrants for a multi-pass campaign, not a one-turn trick. Ledger at pass_74, source at WARRANT#92.
